**Additional Pricing Components                                                                              Standard               Expedited**

**~                                                                                                                                                  Turnaround        Turnaround**

n  Additional charge for proofreading................................................................................ $65.00/hour ............... Rush Fee

n  Additional charge for basic layout/formatting............................................................... $55.00/hour ............... Rush Fee

n  Document Quality Assurance Review............................................................................ $45.00/hour ............... Rush Fee

n  File Preparations/TM Maintenance................................................................................. $65.00/hour................ Rush Fee

n  Project Management..................................................................................10% of overall project cost (0.5 hr. min. @$55/hour)



**~ **


**OTHER SERVICES PRICING:**

n  Graphic design services........................................................................................................................................... Individual Quote

n  In-language recordings............................................................................................................................................. Individual Quote

n  Transcription/translation of audio or videos files................................................................................................. Individual Quote

n  No delivery charge for Fax, E-mail, or U.S. Mail. Additional charge for courier services only.

n  Unless indicated otherwise, a one hour minimum will apply to all hourly services.


 



 




 



 




**DELIVERY GUIDELINES:**

Because the actual number of English words is not known until the source document has been translated, turn-around commitments are based on the estimated number of English words that will be delivered, as determined in LanguageLine’s best judgment before commencing work. Additional services could add extra days to a project timeline.

n  Less than 1,000 words.............................................................................................................................................. 1 - 3 business days

n  1,001 to 2,500 words................................................................................................................................................. 4 - 6 business days

n  2,501 to 7,500 words................................................................................................................................................. 6 - 8 business days

n  Greater than 7,501 words......................................................................................................................................... 8 + business days


 




**~ **


**RUSH FEES:**

A 50% rush charge will apply when an expedited delivery date is requested.

processingrequirements:
(% class="box infomessage" %)
(((
__**Connector Solution: Figma and Contentful **__
)))

1. Client submits via connector, which generates a notification email to the starts team ([[projectstarts@llts.com>>path:mailto:projectstarts@llts.com]]) & Lab (Oussama: [[oabou-jamous@llts.com>>path:mailto:oabou-jamous@llts.com]])
1. When notification is received, Starts creates a new project in XTRF
11. Process workflow: Connector Project
11. Add placeholder file “Connector Translation Request” 
11. Change Type of Project to “Continuous Request”
11. Add to Group
11. Tag @Oussama  in comments to add efforts
1. Lab/Oussama preps and notifies Starts when files are ready
1. Starts assigns a PM
1. PM to Linguist (Translation)
1. PM notifies Lab when files are returned from translation
1. Lab delivers to Client via Connector
1. PM sends Client delivery notification with placeholder file
1. PM closes projects as Delivered and releases payables
1. End of Month - Project Billing adds receivables for the Group/all continuous request projects (Eric is building a view for these “continuous request” monthly project requests) and marks “Ready to review receivables” on each project in group to release to accounting
1. Accounting invoices

__**NO PROOFREADING:**__ This client has routinely rejected quotes with Proofreading in favor of quotes from competitors who do not include PR. We are no longer quoting Proofreading costs to this client, and should not perform proofreading unless it is (a) explicitly quoted, which would be rare and need BDM approval, or (b) the project really requires it and we can fit it into the margin without billing client. If you think your project must have proofreading, please see your vertical lead to confirm with the BDM.

Language independent tasks: unless the project falls under the minimum, please quote all non-linguistic tasks as language independent. The client has had questions about how we calculate formatting and other hours, and has requested that these are shown as a group, rather than per language.

intake:
Quote requests will come in with subject line:

RITMXXXXXX – WWT/LLS Translation Request Quote Required

Intake team will need to download the files for the Quote Starts team before we can provide a quote.

If the files are not attached to the email by the WWT requestor, Intake can request the client send the files via ShareFile link.

Note, client may send using WWT secure log in ([[https:~~/~~/login.wwt.com/>>url:https://protect-us.mimecast.com/s/fu8MCYEmv5ug79riQXpxw?domain=login.wwt.com/]]), at this time both the Intake team ([[translation@llts.com>>path:mailto:translation@llts.com]] or [[translation@llts.com>>path:mailto:translation@llts.com]]) and Project Managers (Nhan Le and Natalya Voznyuk) are unable to successfully log in and download. Please use ShareFile instead. 

(% style="background-color:#f1c40f; color:null" %)Project must be created with "RITMXXXXXX" included in the name.
requirequote:
Most projects will come through Service Now and explicitly request an estimate. 

These estimates must be submitted to client via Service Now following strict guidelines for automatic approval process.

INSTRUCTIONS:
 Estimate and provide quote to client through Service Now by Reply All to their email request.  When creating the project folder ensure that the client’s Remittance **Number or Ticket Number (**RITM~#~#~#~#) is included in the project name. For example, RITM98060.

WWT will use their ServiceNow (SN) system to request translation estimates and send approvals. The WWT requester can either attach files directly to a request email via SN, or use a permanent ShareFile link LL has provided (ShareFile: Natalya, Alex, Nhan and Erin H).


The request will come to:

[[translation@languageline.com>>path:mailto:translation@languageline.com]] – LanguageLine Intake

[[translation@llts.com>>path:mailto:translation@llts.com]] – LanguageLine Intake

[[nvoznyuk@llts.com>>path:mailto:nvoznyuk@llts.com]] – Natalya Voznyuk

[[swing@llts.com>>path:mailto:swing@llts.com]] – Stacy Wing

[[nle@llts.com>>path:mailto:nle@llts.com]] – Nhan Le


The communication work similarly to Basecamp in that you will Reply All to SN and anyone else on copy.


Intake Process: ALL steps must be followed.

(% style="background-color:#f1c40f" %)-     When creating the project folder ensure that the Remittance Number or Ticket Number is included in the project name. For example, RITM98060.

-     Once the Estimate is ready, please do a **Reply All** to the requestor email with an attached PDF Estimate **AND **will also need to include the actual dollar amount in the body of the email (Estimated_price: $350.00). Exact wording is needed: **Estimated_price:**

This is VERY important for approval. Ensure that all logos and graphics are removed from your email signature line. This allows SN to pick-up the needed information, place in the correct area and send internally within WWT for approval.

If LanguageLine has a question for the client and we are not ready to include the price, we would leave the pricing out. We should only use the “Estimated_price:” piece when we are ready to communicate the final estimated price and have it sent to the WWT requesters manager. Otherwise, just reply and cc the requestor with questions to assist in the estimation.



//Sample email to client~://


 Hello Ryan,


 Attached you will find the quote to translate your project #5514082.

 Estimated_price: $99.00


 We appreciate your consideration of our cost proposal. Please let us know if there are any questions or if we can assist in any other way.
intakeinfo:
When FOH sends quotes to WWT their system sends an automated response. These are not approvals as they do not say "RITM~#~## has been approved...."


When we receive an approval sometimes the response will not include the project number but you should be able to find the project using the RITM number. 

Here's an example of an approval: 


[[image:Approval Example.JPG]]

{{{• The completed translations will be returned in the same format they have been provided via secure delivery method 
  electronically.
• Treatment of proper names, acronyms, and other items will be handled on a language-by-language basis to maximize 
  fluency and comprehension according to our professional linguistic discretion.
• World Wide Technology is responsible for all Engineering and post-delivery Quality Assurance steps.
• An in-context linguistic online review is recommended after files are imported to the final format/application and is 
  not included in the project scope. LanguageLine is not responsible for errors introduced during text import, how the 
  text appears online or for any mistranslations resulting from a lack of context during translation; as such issues are 
  identified and corrected as part of the final linguistic quality assurance step.
• Requests to change text or formatting after a project has started will result in additional charges.
• All amounts in this estimate are stated in U.S. Dollars (USD).
• The attached Terms and Conditions are an integral part of this quote.}}}

intake:
**__Connector Projects:__**

Requester: Cheryl Wertz - [[wertzc@dss.nyc.gov>>mailto:wertzc@dss.nyc.gov]]
Technology fee: $0.02/word + per word rate
Monthly fee arrangement (confirmed with Finance team?) N/A
Languages:  Arabic, Bengali, Chinese (Traditional), Haitian Creole, French, Polish, Korean, Russian, Spanish, and Urdu
Workflow:  TRCE only, no OLR (per wiki)
Quote or preapproved: Preapproved (exception is if amount exceeds $5000) 
Do we need a new project for every drop? No, new project should be set up each month. Each drop that comes in during the month will need to be part of that monthly project. 

Additional processing notes

Requests usually are sent to us from Jack Ding or other members of NYC Mayor’s Office for Economic Opportunity with him CC'ed. Requests/emails are identified as "New batch [MM, DD, YYYY] from MOEO/NYCO" and need to be treated as monthly projects. Projects still to be set up under Cheryl

NY Intake gets notified by Oussama Abou-Jamous <[[oabou-jamous@llts.com>>path:mailto:oabou-jamous@llts.com]]>  when files are submitted by the client via Crowdin Enterprise (AKA Connector) platform. Files are prepped and delivered via Connector so we might not see the files in the Lab folders as we normally do.

When creating the projects please use the following naming convention:
EN-10 MOEO x LL connector ([MM.YY])
